method: Per-sign, per-site (LA) / per-fold (LB PC) within-word uniform-permutation null for INITIAL and FINAL slots (p<=0.05, 1000 draws); LA asymmetry = label-swap permutation null on D=mean(init)-mean(final), 5000 draws, two-sided, with Wilcoxon signed-rank cross-check. LB PC = seeded balanced 5-way partition (seed=7) of DĀMOS >=2-sign wordforms; PC PASSED iff mean(final_sig_parts)>=mean(init_sig_parts).

positive_control.split: seeded balanced 5-way (seed=7); contiguous fold assignment after seeded shuffle
positive_control.stability_across_base_seeds: init>final stable for base_seed in {0,11,99} and partition seeds {7,42,123}

key_findings: PC FAILED (frozen gate): on LB the machinery reports mean_init=1.565 >= mean_final=1.246 (direction init>final), stably across base seeds {0,11,99} and partition seeds {7,42,123}. LB is known to be word-FINAL dominated, so per the frozen rule the method is judged to manufacture initial-dominance -> MACHINERY_UNINFORMATIVE.

Mechanism of PC failure (honest): the within-word permutation null flags ANY positional concentration; LB has many highly position-specialized word-INITIAL syllabograms (e.g. A, DA, DI, KA, KU, ME clear all 5 folds at init, final=0) that clear the null in more folds than final-specialized signs. The null is not specific to grammatical-ending structure.
Because the PC gate fired, NO LA claim is made. The LA numbers are reported for transparency only and are NOT a valid inference under this protocol.

For-the-record LA numbers (NOT a claim): mean_init=0.714 vs mean_final=0.619, label-swap p=0.836, Wilcoxon p=0.599, 14 signs init>final vs 16 final>init vs 12 tie -> even setting the PC aside, there is NO detectable positional asymmetry in LA.
Power was adequate: 42 qualifying signs (>=30 occ) and 10 qualifying sites (>=20 words) -> not underpowered; the verdict is driven by the PC, not by power.
successor_hypotheses: H027a: Redesign the PC to test the SPECIFIC structural claim (grammatical endings) rather than whole-inventory positional concentration -- e.g. restrict the LB PC to known inflectional-ending signs, or use a positional-concentration ratio that is symmetric by construction, then re-run the LA asymmetry test.

H027b: Replace the within-word permutation null with a position-frequency-matched null (match each sign's marginal position profile) so that initial-vs-final comparison is not biased by inventory-wide position specialization.

H027c: Test the LA init-vs-final asymmetry with a DIFFERENT, PC-passing statistic (e.g. entropy of the site-distribution of each sign's positional preference) before claiming any LA positional generalization asymmetry.

H027d: Characterize LB's strong word-INITIAL specialization directly (which signs, which folds) as a standalone L2 finding -- it is a real structural property of Mycenaean wordforms that this machinery surfaced, independent of the LA question.

H027e: Re-examine E023 (A-initial generalizes 9/10) and E026 (RO-final at 1/10) under the corrected null; the apparent init-generalizes / final-local contrast may be an artifact of the same initial-bias the PC exposed, and should be re-tested before synthesis.

H027f: Build a position-symmetric control corpus (e.g. shuffle LA word-internal positions only, preserving first AND last slots) to establish a null under which init and final are exchangeable by construction.
non_circular: Sign names reported as opaque tokens; NO phonetic / sound / meaning / language / reading inference drawn. LB used as positive-control benchmark ONLY (no reading transferred to LA). Verdict is mechanical from the frozen PC gate; the operator did not adjudicate. The PC fired honestly and the LA claim was suppressed accordingly.
deviations: Self-check assertion in machinery.py __main__ had an arithmetic error in the expected FINAL-null mean (third toy word [B,A] has P(last==A)=1/2, not 0); corrected before any data run. The prereg/plan_hash were frozen against prereg.md only (machinery.py is not part of the hash), so this fix does not alter the freeze.
Label-swap null used 5000 draws (>=2000 required by protocol).

PC stability was checked across 3 base seeds and 3 partition seeds (all init>final) to confirm the PC failure is not a seed artifact; this check is diagnostic only and does not change the frozen verdict.
